name: Eloking
description: A consumer-focused boosting and coaching marketplace selling rank boosts, duo sessions, and coaching for competitive games (LoL, Valorant, CS2, Dota 2, etc.).

details.targetMarket: Competitive gamers who want rank boosting, duo boosting, or one-off coaching (players of League of Legends, Valorant, CS2, Dota 2, Rocket League, etc.)

items: Per-order rank boosting (division boosts, placement boosts, win boosts)
One-off coaching sessions and booster-led mentoring
Support for multiple games: League of Legends, Valorant, CS2, Dota 2, Rocket League, Overwatch 2, etc.
Booster profiles with language, specialties and reviews
Price calculators per game/order (dynamic pricing based on rank, server, add-ons)
Secure payments via Stripe (end-to-end encrypted checkout)
Automatic order assignment to boosters with optional booster selection

missingFeatures: No standardized, subscription-style coaching packages or recurring billing for ongoing skill development (coaching is sold as one-off sessions), which prevents predictable ARPU from serious learners.
Lack of publicly verifiable per-booster performance metrics (no linked match history, replay proof, or mandatory livestream records) — makes objective quality comparison between boosters difficult.
No integrated recorded-session delivery or built-in replay-analysis tooling for coaching customers — progress tracking and deliverables are informal.

Pricing is highly custom per-order with many variables and regional differences but no clear standardized price tables for common boosts (e.g., Silver->Gold), making price comparison and conversion optimization harder for buyers.
No visible B2B or team/academy offerings or API for partners, limiting expansion into esports orgs, coaching academies, or platform integrations.
No explicit escrow/milestone workflow for high-value orders (payments appear collected up-front via Stripe), which is an opening for fraud-protection or escrow-first competitors.

pricingPlans.name: Per-order boost
pricingPlans.note: Entry-level order price shown on product pages; final price is calculated per-order by the website calculator and varies by game, rank, server and add-ons.

pricingPlans.name: Coaching / Session
pricingPlans.note: Coaching offered as one-off sessions or ad-hoc packages priced per booster; no public fixed coaching subscription shown.

pricingEvidence.label: CS2 rank boost product page
pricingEvidence.claim: Product page states a CS2 boost price can be as low as 1.49 EUR and that final cost is calculated by the on-site price calculator.

pricingEvidence.label: LoL pricing blog
pricingEvidence.claim: Eloking's blog explains LoL boosting prices vary widely by boost type and rank and gives an example average price per division in Diamond.

companyEvidence.label: About page
companyEvidence.claim: Eloking describes itself as an official company (Eloking Ltd.) and explains the company background and focus on boosting & coaching.

companyEvidence.label: Company registry (Lursoft) entry
companyEvidence.claim: Public company data shows Eloking (SIA) registration details in Latvia, supporting the company's country of registration.

companyEvidence.label: Crunchbase profile
companyEvidence.claim: Crunchbase lists Eloking and summarizes it as a platform for professional players to provide boosting and coaching services worldwide.

reviewEvidence.label: Trustpilot reviews
reviewEvidence.claim: Trustpilot hosts >1,400 reviews for Eloking, showing many positive reviews but also some negative ones about order delays or service issues.

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit complaint thread
reviewEvidence.claim: User reports on Reddit describe long completion times and rude/unprofessional booster interactions for Eloking orders.

featureEvidence.label: Booster profile (example)
featureEvidence.claim: Booster profile pages list booster specialties, languages, and include 'Calculate Boost Price' and customer feedback, demonstrating the marketplace-style booster listings.

featureEvidence.label: CS2 product page
featureEvidence.claim: Product page highlights features like 'Play with Booster' add-on, secure Stripe payments, and on-site price calculator for dynamic quotes.

featureEvidence.label: Contact / refund policy mention
featureEvidence.claim: Contact page and site copy state flexible refund options (refunds if order not started, mid-way stops) and 24/7 live chat support.

strengths: Wide game coverage and product breadth — supports major competitive titles (LoL, Valorant, CS2, Dota 2, Rocket League), increasing addressable market.
Dynamic on-site price calculator and low entry price (example: €1.49), allowing rapid purchase decisions for low-cost orders.
Strong customer-facing operations: 24/7 live chat, explicit refund flexibility, and visible booster profiles that reduce friction in ordering.
Noticeable third‑party footprint (Trustpilot reviews, SEO’d blog content) which drives organic trust and discovery in a price‑sensitive market.
weaknesses: Inconsistent delivery & quality: community threads and Trustpilot include reports of long completion times, multiple booster reassignments, and rude communication — indicating variable operational quality.
Low transparency on verifiable booster outcomes: the site does not expose linked match replays or mandatory livestream proof per order, making objective verification hard for buyers.
Pricing complexity and lack of standardized packages: highly variable per-order pricing without clear published tables for common flows creates comparison friction and may deter conversions.

Legal/ethical exposure: boosting services contravene many game ToS; reliance on this model can pose long-term regulatory/partner risks and can lead to potential account-safety concerns despite site claims of safety.
